explanations.en: This action ranks first because it fits the city's preferred timeframe very well and has very strong policy, legal, and mitigation feasibility. Its main drawback is that the expected emissions reduction is limited, and it does not match the city's preferred sector.

This action stays near the top because it also fits the city's preferred timeframe and has strong policy support, plus it supports a preferred co-benefit. It ranks below the first action because its emissions reduction is limited and it does not match the city's preferred sector.

This action is placed third because it has very strong timing and policy support and supports a preferred co-benefit. It sits below the top two because its expected emissions reduction is very limited and it does not match the city's preferred sector.

This action ranks fourth because it matches the city's preferred sector and timeframe, and it also supports both preferred co-benefits. It does not rank higher because its expected emissions reduction is very limited in the current city inventory.
